pi_items.source: Task instructions and verifier.py logic explicitly enforce this exact string for the target alert.
Splunk documentation and verifier.py logic confirm these are valid representations for exactly 24 hours of suppression.
Splunk default field extractions for linux_secure sourcetype and verifier.py logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on authentic real-world security log data (SSH brute-force) sourced from SecRepo and Loghub, ingested into Splunk. The expected configuration values (alert name "Throttled_SSH_Brute_Force", suppression fields like "src_ip", and 24-hour time expressions) accurately reflect Splunk's administrative requirements and match the explicit constraints of the verifier logic.
